resources.string: The crown breaks the neck, yet the ruler smiles beneath its weight.

The hall answers with ten thousand throats. Subjects and enemies kneel in identical rows, and the throne rises from rival skulls, shattered steel, and endless praise. Every command becomes law before its echo dies. Reality waits outside, torn and pleading, but why serve it when the dream is flawless? The crown is heavy because humility was its price, and that price was gladly paid.
